はぁ～～、いい香り～。<br>やっぱり紅茶は落ち着くね～～♪: Aaah, this smells wonderful! Tea really is so relaxing!
……いつモンスターが来るかも分からない大穴の中で、<br>わざわざお湯を沸かして:
...In the Abyss, where monsters could appear at any moment, you
actually go to the trouble of boiling water for tea...?
え～？ キミは大穴で探索している時は、お茶の時間を作らないの？: Huh? You don't have tea time when you're exploring the Abyss?
休憩は取ることもあるっちゃあるが……。<br>少なくとも周囲の危険がないことを確:
I do take breaks now and then... But only after I've made sure there's
no danger around.
そう言われてもね～。<br>ワタシのティータイムはもう、習慣みたいなものだし。: Even so... My tea time is already a habit, you know.
（ティータイムが習慣か……。やっぱりこいつ、意外に育ちがいいのか？<br>紅茶を:
(Tea time as a habit... So she's surprisingly well-bred after all? Her
tea-pouring is practiced too. She's no ordinary adventurer.)
……なあ、メリッサ。<br>お前はなんで、トレジャーハンターなんてやってるんだ？: Hey, Melissa... Why are you a treasure hunter of all things?
え？ そんなの、儲かるからに決まってるじゃん。<br>一攫千金はトレジャーハンタ:
Huh? Obviously because it pays well! Striking it rich is a treasure
hunter's dream! You can never have too much money!
まあ、それはそうだが。それにしたって、危険を冒してまで<br>財宝を狙うからには:
Well, that's true. But still, if you're going to risk danger for treasure,
there's got to be a real reason, right?
えー、なになに～？ キミ、ワタシにそんなに興味あるんだ？<br>ひょっとして、ひ:
Oh? What's this? You're that interested in me? Could it be love at
first sight?
ワタシの家の婿に入ろうっていうなら、<br>それなりの品格がないとお父様が納得し:
If you're thinking of marrying into my family, you'll need some class
or my father won't approve. You think you're up to it?
あ～……もう、そういうのはいいんだっけ。<br>ごめん、なんでもないや。: Ah... wait, I shouldn't talk about that. Sorry, forget it.
（婿に、格……か。<br>……まるで名家の息女みたいな物言いだな）:
(A son-in-law, and class... Sounds just like something a daughter of a
noble family would say.)
ま、そういうのはさておいて～。はい、キミの分の紅茶とスコーンだよ。<br>特別に:
Well, anyway. Here, your tea and scone. I'm sharing since we've got a
long way ahead. Need to fuel up, right?
……そうだな。何を暢気にとは思ったが、お前の言う通りだ。<br>２人で協力してい:
...You're right. I thought you were being too carefree, but you have a
point. Since we're in this together, we need to be in top form.
そういうこと～♪<br>ほらほらっ！ 遠慮しなくていいからね♪: That's right! Here, go ahead! Don't hold back!
いただくとしよう。……うん、これはいい茶葉を使ってるな。<br>蒸らしかたも十分:
Let me have some... Yeah, these are good tea leaves. The steeping is
perfect, too.
おっ、分かる？ いけるクチだねぇ。<br>味の分かる人にはごちそうのしがいもある:
Oh, you can tell? You've got good taste. It's rewarding to treat
someone who knows quality.
スコーンもサクサクしていて美味いな。<br>ほどよい甘みで、疲れも吹っ飛ぶよ。:
The scones are flaky and great. Just the right sweetness—it knocks
the fatigue right out of you.
でしょ～？ いいお店、知ってるんだ～。<br>それじゃあ……はいっ。: Right? I know a good shop. So then... here you go!
紅茶とスコーンの代金、ちょうだいしま～～す♪: Pay up for the tea and scones!♪
もっちろん！<br>儲けるチャンスは逃さないのが、ワタシの信条さ～♪:
You bet! I never let a chance to make some money slip by—that's my
motto!♪
えーっと、三叉路を直進してから１００歩分まっすぐ行って、<br>２つ目の横穴に入:
Okay, from the fork we went straight for a hundred paces, then into
the second side tunnel... yep, we're almost there!
……って、うっわ！ ここの道、泥だらけになってる！<br>地下水が流れ込んできた:
Whoa, this path is all muddy! Underground water must have flooded it.
I really don't want to get dirty...
探索中なんだから、少々の汚れなんて気にしてる余裕ないだろ。<br>ホラ、行くぞ！:
We're in the middle of exploring. We can't be bothered with a little
dirt. Come on, let's go!
えー、靴とか服とか汚れるのやだよ～。<br>あ、そうだ！ キミがおんぶしてくれた:
Aww, I don't want to get my shoes and clothes dirty. Oh, I know! What
if you give me a piggyback ride? Then I won't get dirty, right?
あははっ、冗談冗談～。キミ、いい反応するよね～。<br>からかい甲斐があるよ。ク:
Ahaha, just kidding! You have such great reactions. So easy to tease.
Heh heh heh...
……お？ あそこにある宝箱は……！<br>間違いない！ 情報通りだな！:
Oh! That chest over there...! No mistake! It's just like the information
said!
え……？ ちょっと待って、キミに聞いた情報が確かなら、<br>目的地はまだ少し先:
Huh...? Hold on, if the information you heard was correct, the
destination should still be a little further ahead...
情報に誤差があったんだろう。問題ない。<br>ようやく見つけたぞ！ あれが夢にま:
There must have been an error in the information. No problem. I
finally found it! That's the treasure I've dreamed of!
あっ、ちょっと！<br>不用意に開けたら、何があるか……！: Ah, wait! If you open it without caution, something might jump out...!
メリッサの制止を無視し、<user>は宝箱を開ける。<br>その直後――:
Ignoring Melissa's protests, the Commander opened the chest. The
very next instant—
何っ……！？ 宝箱がモンスターに……！？: What! The chest is a monster!
いけないっ、ミミックだ！<br>逃げて～～！: Oh no, it's a Mimic! Run!
あ、危なかった……！ メリッサの呼びかけがなければ、<br>今頃、腕を噛み切られ:
*phew*, that was close...! If you hadn't called out, Melissa, I could've
had my arm bitten off!
完全にこっちを敵とみなしてるね……。<br>ここはひとまず……逃げよう！: It definitely sees us as enemies now... Let's get out of here!
ふぅ……この岩陰にいれば、ひとまず姿は隠せそうだね。: *phew*... We should be able to hide behind this rock for now.
すまん、メリッサ。俺のせいで、お前まで危険な目にあわせてしまった。<br>詫びの:
I'm sorry, Melissa. Because of me, you got caught up in danger too. I
can't apologize enough...
そんなこと気にしなくていいって。<br>困っている人を助けるのは、騎士として当然:
Don't worry about it. As a knight, helping people in need is only
natural.
騎士……！？ お前、騎士だったのか？<br>じゃあ、トレジャーハンターってのは…:
A knight! You were a knight? Then what's the treasure hunter thing
all about?
ハハハ、口が滑っちゃったなぁ……。<br>まあ、絶対に人に知られたくないこと、っ:
Hahaha, I let that slip... Well, it's not like it's a huge secret or
anything.
それじゃあ、本当に騎士の家の出なのか……。<br>それがどうしてトレジャーハンタ:
So you really are from a knightly family... Then why are you doing
something as flighty as treasure hunting?
……ま、バレちゃったからには、隠してもしょうがないよね。: Well, now that the cat's out of the bag, there's no point in hiding it.
お察しの通り、ワタシの実家はミレスガルドじゃ割と有名な名家だったんだ。<br>立:
As you guessed, my family was a famous noble house in Milesgard.
They trained me Spartan-style to become a proper knight.
鍛練は厳しかったけど、ワタシも嫌じゃなかったしね。騎士ってカッコいいし。<br>:
The training was strict, but I didn't hate it. Knights are cool, you
know? But before I could become one, my family fell into ruin.
とんでもない額の借金ができたせいで、家族みんなで貧乏生活。<br>こうなったらも:
We ended up with a huge debt and the whole family in poverty. So I
had to give up being a knight and go for quick riches.
……っていうわけで、トレジャーハンターになったってわけ。: And that's how I ended up as a treasure hunter.
なるほど……事情は分かったが、それにしたってずいぶん思い切りがいいな。<br>…:
I see... I understand the circumstances, but that's quite a decisive
move. Didn't you have any second thoughts?
道が１つダメになったからって、落ち込んでる場合じゃないでしょ。<br>それに、騎:
It's not like I could mope because one path closed. Besides, I admired
knights, but was never good at rules and order.
なってみたら、案外トレジャーハンターの方が自由で向いてたみたい。<br>今は毎日:
I tried the treasure hunter life, and it surprisingly suited me better.
Now I have fun every day. Hand-to-mouth isn't so bad, you know!
すごいポジティブだな……。けど、納得ができたよ。<br>どうりで、行動や仕草の端:
You're seriously positive... but now I understand. No wonder I could
sense your upbringing in every action and gesture.
それに、体力も筋力も十分あるみたいだしな。<br>お前のその肉体……活かさない手:
Plus, you clearly have stamina and strength. I'd be a fool not to put
that body of yours to use.
肉体！？ いや～ん、エッチ～！<br>言っとくけど身体を売る気なんてないからね！:
My body! Eek, you perv! I'll have you know I have no intention of
selling myself!
違うわっ！ 騎士としての鍛錬を積んだやつがいて、<br>頼りになるって言ってんだ:
No, that's not it! I'm saying it's a relief to have someone with knight
training around!
あはははっ、冗談だってば。<br>で……その様子だと、何か考えがあるみたいだね。:
Ahaha, just kidding. So... from that look, you've got something in
mind, huh?
ああ。俺の不手際でミミックに追われることになったからな。<br>２人だけであれを:
Yeah. Since it's my fault we got chased by that Mimic. I was thinking
it'd be too dangerous for just the two of us to take it on, but...
お前が騎士なら話は別だ。目的を変更して、ミミックをこの場で何とかしたい。<br>:
But if you're a knight, that changes things. Let's take care of that
Mimic here. It could attack others. Help?
宝は諦めてでも、あとから来る人たちのために、ってことか……。<br>へぇ、キミ、:
So you'd give up the treasure for the people who come after... Huh,
you've got some good points after all.
ワタシにも一応、騎士の血が流れてるからね。<br>キミの心意気、乗ってやろうじゃ:
I've got knight's blood flowing through me, you know. I'm with you!
Let's beat that Mimic together!
そう言ってくれると思ったよ。<br>さっそく作戦を聞いてくれ。まずは――: I knew you'd say that. Alright, here's the plan. First—
